text:
western pa

kinda middle of nowhere tbh

yeah

mostly boring but also yeah ppl are getting squeezed bad

like everything costs more and theres nothing here except warehouses and shitty jobs

feels like nobody cares unless ur already doing fine i guess

probably the no future part

money too obviously but its like even if you do everything ur supposed to it doesnt really go anywhere

just work sleep work sleep

and yeah nobody really sees ppl like us unless they wanna make fun of it or use it for votes or whatever

idk maybe thats dramatic but thats how it feels

probably the whole system i guess

politicians talk like they care then nothing changes

companies come in and act like theyre saving the area then pay like shit anyway

feels like its all set up for other people not us

idk maybe thats obvious but yeah

yeah mostly ppl from towns like this

working ppl too i guess

like regular ppl that do what theyre told and still get nowhere

feels like some ppl get cushioned from everything and the rest of us just eat it

probably all of that

money and connections obviously

but yeah also feels like they look down on regular ppl and act like we're stupid if we notice it

like if you say anything you're just bitter or lazy or whatever

idk maybe ppl are just tired of being talked down to

more just years of it i think

like after i dropped out and started working full time it kinda set in

everywhere online its all "learn to code" or move or hustle harder or whatever

like alright cool with what money

just starts feeling like if ur not already on track nobody really cares what happens to you

yeah

more checked out at first probably

then angry i guess

mostly just wanted to find ppl that actually get it and arent doing the fake motivational bs

like just someone being real about it for once

mostly online tbh

not really ppl around here

discord servers reddit youtube comments whatever

sometimes you can tell whos actually dealing with this shit and whos just performing

i guess i trust ppl more when they just say it straight and dont try to spin everything into some inspirational lesson or politician crap

probably that they actually understand it

and yeah maybe they say the quiet part out loud too

like not even in some crazy way just... not pretending everythings fine when it obviously isnt

if somebody sounds angry but its real i trust that more than fake optimism tbh

mostly that nobody in charge actually plans to make life better for ppl like us

like they know its bad and just manage it

maybe ignored yeah

sometimes replaced is how ppl talk about it online but idk

mostly feels like regular ppl are just disposable now i guess

probably wanting to understand who benefits i guess

like i still pull back a lot tbh but you start wondering why it keeps happening the same way

who actually gets something out of ppl being tired broke and isolated all the time

feels like thats not an accident maybe

idk maybe im overthinking it but yeah

probably institutions and elites i guess

like ppl with money media politics all tied together somehow

same types always seem fine no matter what happens

and if regular ppl get mad they just call them stupid or dangerous or whatever

kinda feels protected yeah

probably both

like i dont trust the super polished "explainer" type stuff that much

but if someone can break it down in a way that actually matches real life then yeah

and sometimes the deeper agenda stuff kinda makes more sense than the official version tbh

not even saying every theory online is true or whatever

just feels like theres always more going on than what they admit upfront

yeah probably all that

like if it explains who benefits and why the same stuff keeps happening then it feels more real

especially if it lines up with patterns youve already noticed

otherwise its just another official story with nicer branding

i guess ppl just want some reason that actually fits why regular ppl keep getting screwed over the same way every time

probably that they make you feel like youre not crazy for noticing it

like independent matters too and saying unpopular stuff i guess

but mostly just somebody being like nah youre not imagining this

that hits harder than all the polished expert crap tbh

yeah

probably all of that tbh

like if theyve actually lived it or at least talk like a real person and not some HR robot

and yeah not judging the anger matters

mostly the belonging thing tho i guess

like if it feels like youre not just some bitter loser for noticing whats obvious

if it feels like theres actually other ppl seeing the same shit

then yeah i open up more

idk

probably ppl who are fed up in general first

but also yeah ppl with kinda the same background maybe

working class small town whatever

ppl who can see where things are going and dont act like youre insane for being worried about it

that feels more real i guess

probably the rules changing against you i guess

and yeah maybe ppl from backgrounds like mine just matter less now

like not even in some dramatic movie way

just slow enough that if you say it out loud ppl act like youre whining

and then nobody stands up for you unless they want something out of it

so yeah it kinda feels like by the time anyone admits somethings wrong its already too late

probably ppl with a bigger picture i guess

like anger helps if its real but just being mad isnt enough

if someone can actually connect it to whats been happening for years and why regular ppl keep getting pushed aside then yeah i listen more

otherwise it just turns into ranting

idk i guess im just tired of ppl acting like everythings random when it clearly keeps landing on the same people over and over

probably working class ppl in general first

but places like mine yeah you notice it more i guess

small towns get gutted then ppl act shocked when everyones bitter

and maybe certain values too

like if you still think stability community family basic stuff should matter then you kinda get treated like youre backwards or something

idk maybe thats just how it feels online but yeah

yeah probably both

like the culture part feels real online all the time

everything normal gets framed like its embarrassing or evil or whatever

but also idk it does kinda feel useful for ppl in charge if everyone's divided and too defensive to notice whats actually happening

like if you make regular ppl hate each other then nobody looks up

so yeah i guess both

probably the kind of life i thought was supposed to be respectable

like work a job keep ur head down maybe have a family someday

nothing crazy

and now it feels like that whole thing gets talked about like its pathetic or naive or secretly bad somehow

being from a small town and working class is part of it too i guess

but mostly its like the basic deal doesnt even count anymore

probably media/culture people first

but institutions back it up i guess

like one group mocks it and the other makes sure it doesnt really work anymore

and then the broader crowd just repeats it cause thats what everyones trained to do online

idk

just feels coordinated sometimes even if nobody says it out loud like that

probably making ppl like me feel ashamed of wanting a normal life

that part feels weirdly deliberate yeah

like if you can make somebody feel lame or backward for wanting basic stuff then they kinda police themselves

and then yeah they stay isolated too

like everyone probably thinks theyre the only one noticing it so nobody says anything

idk

maybe thats the point i guess

probably all 3

some ppl just give up yeah  
some get angry and weird about it

but mostly i think they start looking for anyone who still says nah thats worth defending actually

like just hearing that from someone feels different now

makes you feel less crazy i guess

yeah

mostly that last part i guess

like lived it helps and pushing back matters too

but if they make it feel like ppl like me still actually have a place then yeah that feels real

cause most of the time it just feels like youre being phased out and told to smile about it

so when someone actually says nah you matter too

idk that sticks with you a lot more

probably belonging first

then honesty i guess

like if somebody can actually say whats happening without all the fake safe wording and also make it feel like youre not alone in seeing it

yeah that gets me way more interested

cause most ppl either lie to you or act like youre defective for even noticing anything

so if someone can name it clearly and not do that

idk yeah i'd listen

probably dignity i guess

betrayed too

but dignity hits harder

like just the idea that wanting a normal decent life shouldnt make you feel small or obsolete or whatever

and yeah holding onto who you are matters too if it feels like everythings trying to blur that out

idk

those words sound less fake than all the usual stuff

probably the sense that ppl like me deserve respect and a future too

like background and values are part of it i guess

but mostly just not being treated like youre already obsolete

or like the life you wanted is some joke

idk

just wanna feel like theres still a place for ppl like us without having to apologize for it

yeah

both probably

small town working class ppl for sure

but also anyone who still wants a decent normal life and feels stupid for even saying that out loud now

like job family some kind of stability

shouldnt be this embarrassing thing

idk

feels like theres a lot more of us than ppl admit maybe

probably the way ppl hold back til they know they wont get mocked for it

like one on one ppl say way more than they ever would in public or online

and online too you can kinda see the pattern under all the fake irony and posting

ppl are careful til they know youre not gonna instantly call them some kind of freak

then suddenly theyre like yeah ok finally someone said it

so idk

it kinda feels bigger than it looks on the surface

probably being labeled a bad person tbh

like mocked sucks yeah and being cut off too

but once ppl decide what you are before you even explain anything thats kinda it

then every word gets twisted after that

so most ppl just shut up

or they do the whole joke/irony thing so they can back out if they need to

idk

it makes everyone talk in code almost

probably the chance to still be heard at all

like job and relationships matter obviously

reputation too i guess

but if you think saying one wrong thing means nobody even tries to hear you honestly then yeah you start filtering everything

and after a while you barely even know how to say what you mean straight

idk

it makes ppl feel fake all the time

probably if it feels like theyre actually risking something by saying it

like not just farming outrage or doing the same safe edgy act for views

and if they talk like they actually know ppl like me exist and arent just using us as a prop or whatever

idk you can kinda tell when someones just performing

but when they say stuff straight and it lines up with what youve noticed for a while

yeah i trust that more

especially if they dont instantly backpedal the second someone gets mad at them

probably that last one first

like youve been lied to and pushed aside yeah maybe

but if someone just flat out says ppl like you still matter and dont have to disappear quietly

that hits harder

cause its not even just anger then

its like ok maybe im not insane for feeling this way

maybe theres actually still a place for us

idk

that gets through way more than just rage stuff

probably being part of something i guess

like being understood matters first yeah

but after that

idk you kinda want it to be more than just venting into the void

like actual ppl who get it and dont act like youre broken for wanting normal stuff

some sense of dignity or whatever

and maybe a future that doesnt feel fake

yeah that sounds better than just being alone with it all the time

yeah

belonging first probably

then maybe making sense of it

like if its just random venting or doomposting thats whatever

but if its ppl who actually get it and youre not constantly having to explain the whole thing from scratch

that matters a lot

and yeah ppl standing up for each other too i guess

idk

just feels like most of the time youre on your own unless you find the right corner of the internet or something

yeah

probably the sense that they actually take ppl like me seriously

like the way ppl talk matters too and values obviously

but if it still has that vibe where you know youre one wrong word away from getting treated like a problem then its not really different

the right spaces feel different cause you dont have to do the whole disclaimer thing first

ppl just kinda get it

idk

thats rare tho

probably the feeling that ppl like you are being quietly pushed out

like the anger comes after that i think

and the loss of respect is part of it too

but that whole phased out feeling is the thing underneath all of it

like youre still here obviously but every message is basically move over shut up adapt whatever

idk

when someone just gets that without making you do a whole essay first

yeah that stands out alot

yeah

all of it probably

but morally maybe hits the weirdest

like economic stuff is obvious enough
jobs suck pays trash everythings more expensive

culturally too yeah

but the part that messes with your head is when it feels like youre supposed to apologize just for being the kind of person you are

for wanting basic stuff

for not talking the right way

for coming from where you come from

idk

thats the part that makes ppl go quiet i think

probably being made to feel ashamed

like guilty too yeah

but shame is the thing thats always just kinda there

like youre supposed to look at your whole life and background and way of talking and be like yeah my bad i should probably disappear and let better ppl handle things

idk

and if you push back even a little then it turns into see this is why nobody likes you

so ppl just shut down

or act like they dont care

but they do obviously

yeah

probably the feeling of being seen first

and relief too

like just hearing someone say youre not crazy or lesser for wanting a normal life

that lands hard

cause most of the time it feels like youre already condemned before you even open your mouth

so when somebody cuts through that and doesnt do the usual smug thing

idk

it sticks with you

and yeah then maybe you start thinking ppl like us dont actually have to just accept getting pushed aside forever

yeah

probably both

but shared identity maybe matters more after a point

like words help cause most of the time youre just sitting there thinking this stuff in fragments

and then someone says it straight and youre like oh ok so its not just me

but if theres no actual us behind it then it just turns into more content i guess

more clips more posts more outrage

idk

shared identity makes it feel real

like maybe theres actually ppl who havent completely folded or disappeared yet

yeah

values and way of life probably

background and class are part of it obviously
like that matters too

but its more the feeling that some ppl still notice whats getting stripped away
and dont pretend its progress just cause youre supposed to clap for it

idk

way of life sounds corny maybe
but i think thats what it is

just basic stuff
family
stability
being able to say what you mean without acting like youre evil for it

and yeah maybe being the ppl who still notice whats being lost too

that part definitely feels real

yeah

probably all mixed together

but family and stability first i guess

cause if you cant even have that then what are ppl supposed to be working toward

and being able to speak plain without getting shamed matters too

same with feeling like ppl like you still count at all

idk

just feels like basic stuff shouldnt feel this out of reach or controversial or whatever

yeah

probably the values they refuse to give up

like the way they live matters too i guess
but lots of ppl live regular lives and still act embarrassed by it or cave instantly

the part that feels different is when someone just says no actually this should survive
this still matters
and doesnt say it like theyre apologizing

idk

that feels more like my people than just demographics or whatever

like ppl who havent totally given up on basic decent normal life being worth defending at all

yeah

that they talk like theres still a future for ppl like me probably

shared values matter
and yeah it helps if theyve felt pushed aside too

but the future part is what makes it hit

cause otherwise its just another person describing the problem really well and then thats it

if someone talks like nah youre not just here to be managed or replaced or shamed into disappearing

like theres actually still a place for you and ppl like you

idk

that gets through way more than most stuff

yeah

probably that they can name whats threatening it

like if they cant even say what the problem is then it just sounds fake

standing with ppl like you matters too yeah
and making it feel like theres actually a real us not just random isolated guys online

but naming it straight matters alot

otherwise it turns into that same vague hopeful bs everyone does

idk

if someone can actually say why ppl feel pushed out
and says it without doing the whole apology disclaimer routine

then it starts to feel more real maybe

yeah

institutions probably

like cultural pressure is real too but that usually comes from somewhere
or gets reinforced by somewhere

and yeah you notice who gets protected and who doesnt
obviously

but if you cant talk about the institutions part then it all stays at the level of vibes and posts and ppl arguing in circles

idk

thats kinda the thing that gets me
like it always feels coordinated in this soft fake way
not even like some movie conspiracy thing
just every system somehow leaning the same direction
media jobs schools hr all of it

and then if you notice it too much youre the problem somehow

so yeah

naming that straight matters alot
